problem_addressed: Current computer-aided diagnosis systems use oversimplified one-hot labels that ignore diagnostic uncertainty, forcing models to make overconfident predictions especially with noisy or ambiguous medical images.
core_claim: Leveraging neural network training dynamics to generate calibrated uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels improves multi-view uncertainty estimation and robustness in medical image classification.
key_contributions: Introducing a framework that uses neural network training dynamics (NNTD) to assess sample difficulty and generate uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels
Developing calibrated pseudo-label methods (Pseudo-T and Pseudo-D) that improve model calibration through temperature scaling and Dirichlet calibration
Demonstrating superior performance on echocardiography classification tasks with improved calibration, selective classification, and multi-view fusion
novelty: Unlike prior works that use simple temperature scaling or ensemble approaches, this method specifically leverages the evolution of model predictions across training epochs to quantify sample difficulty and generate uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels, with Pseudo-D introducing class-wise scaling rather than global temperature scaling.
method_summary: The method records model predictions across training epochs for each sample, aggregates these predictions into pseudo-labels representing model uncertainty, calibrates these pseudo-labels using validation data through temperature scaling or Dirichlet calibration, and trains models on these uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels to enhance robustness.
method_components: Neural network training dynamics (NNTD) aggregation using averaged logits across epochs
Temperature scaling (Pseudo-T) for basic calibration using validation cross-entropy minimization
Dirichlet calibration (Pseudo-D) with class-wise scaling matrix and bias using regularization
key_innovation: Using the evolution of model predictions during training (rather than just final predictions) to quantify sample difficulty and generate uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels that reflect the ambiguity encountered during learning.
assumptions: The model's predictions during training reflect the inherent difficulty of each sample
Calibration on validation set generalizes to training set
Averaging logits across epochs provides a stable representation of model uncertainty
theoretical_grounding: The method builds on prior work in knowledge distillation and calibration, using cross-entropy minimization for temperature scaling and regularization terms to prevent overfitting in Dirichlet calibration. No explicit theoretical proofs are provided in the abstract/intro sections.
hyperparameters: R(2+1)D model with Kinetics400 initialization, ADAM optimizer, learning rate 1e-4 for 20 epochs, random rotation ±15°, cropping ratio 0.7, temperature parameters γ, A, b tuned via SGD (lr 0.01, λ1=λ2=1)
headline_result: Pseudo-D achieves best study-level AURC of 0.885 and MAE of 0.180
ablations: Pseudo-T vs Pseudo-D comparison shows Pseudo-D's class-wise calibration improves performance
improvement_over_baseline: Pseudo-D improves study-level MAE by 19.6% (0.224→0.180) and AURC by 3.3% (0.852→0.885) over Vanilla baseline
unstated_weaknesses: Lack of specificity on how neural network training dynamics are quantified and aggregated
No mention of computational efficiency or scalability
No discussion of performance across different medical imaging modalities
threats_to_validity: Potential overfitting to specific types of medical images or conditions
Uncertainty estimates might not generalize across different medical domains
Method's performance under varying levels of label noise isn't specified
open_questions: How does the method perform with different neural network architectures?
What is the computational overhead compared to standard pseudo-labeling methods?
How does the method scale with larger datasets?
future_work: Extending to other medical imaging modalities
gaps_this_enables: More nuanced pseudo-labeling in semi-supervised medical image classification
Better handling of diagnostic uncertainty in medical AI systems
Potential for more robust models under label noise conditions
relevance_to_topic: Directly relevant as it introduces uncertainty-aware pseudo-labels for medical image classification
best_result: Pseudo-D achieves best study-level AURC of 0.885 and MAE of 0.180
title: Pseudo-D: Informing Multi-View Uncertainty Estimation with Calibrated Neural Training Dynamics
